lede: A look at July 10 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Moo Deng and Aleksandar Tunchev, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Volkswagen Beetle
events.desc: The final Volkswagen Beetle rolls off the line in Puebla, Mexico; the last of 5,961 "Special Edition" cars will be exhibited in a museum.

events.title: Association football
events.desc: In women's association football, the United States defeats China in a penalty shoot-out at the Rose Bowl near Los Angeles to win the final match of the 1999 FIFA Women's World Cup. Watched by 90,185 spectators, the final sets a new world record for attendance at a women's sporting event.

events.title: Miguel Ángel Blanco
events.desc: Miguel Ángel Blanco, a member of Partido Popular (Spain), is kidnapped (and later murdered) in the Basque city of Ermua by ETA members, sparking widespread protests.

events.title: Moktar Ould Daddah
events.desc: President Moktar Ould Daddah of Mauritania is ousted in a bloodless coup d'état.

events.title: Day of the Foundation of the Republic
events.desc: The official establishment of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea (North Korea).: 121

events.title: Villa
events.desc: The then-villa of Mayagüez, Puerto Rico, formally receives its city charter from the Royal Crown of Spain.

events.title: Anglo-Spanish War (1654–1660)
events.desc: Anglo-Spanish War (1654–1671): Notable Buccaneer Henry Morgan with an English Privateer force lands at Porto Bello in an attempt to capture the fortified and lucrative Spanish city.

events.title: English Civil War
events.desc: English Civil War: The Battle of Langport takes place.

events.title: Lady Jane Grey
events.desc: Lady Jane Grey takes the throne of England.

events.title: Zhu Chenhao
events.desc: Zhu Chenhao declares the Ming dynasty's Zhengde Emperor a usurper, beginning the Prince of Ning rebellion, and leads his army north in an attempt to capture Nanjing.

births.name: Rajnath Singh
births.line: Indian Politician and Union Home Minister of India

observances.text: Christian feast day: Canute IV of Denmark
Christian feast day: Victoria, Anatolia, and Audax
Independence Day (Bahamas), celebrates the independence of the Bahamas from the United Kingdom in 1973.
